name: Póvoa de Varzim
approachNote: WPT 41°21'·48N 08°46'·73W, 030°/1M to hbr ent. Follow SHM buoys (marking a shoal near the S bkwtr) round to the marina. Tidal streams are weak but there is an appreciable current, mainly S-going, along the coast.
shelter: Good in all winds, but ent is rough in heavy swell and becomes impracticable when wave height >3m. In particular give a wide berth to the head of the N mole where there may be broken water. Reports of larger yachts being denied entry to the marina with bad weather approaching.

marina.name: Marina de Póvoa de Varzim
marina.notes: 241 inc some €1-65; 18m max LOA. Good facilities, many yachts lay up here. M-F 0900-1200, 1400-1700. At the FV hbr. HM Ch 09.

name: Leixões
approachNote: WPT 41°10'·09N 08°42'·26W, to inner mole hds. Leça lt ho, 1·7M N of hbr ent, on a brg of 350·5° leads into inner hbr; thence track 015° to marina. Ent is usually simple. Keep at least 200m off the N mole hd (Quebra-mar) due to a sunken bkwtr (2·5m) and a wreck (8m) 100m SW and S of it.
shelter: Good in marina, but scend enters in gales. The port may be closed occas in strong W'lies and/or swell, as shown by tfc sigs on a mast at the Hbr Office close NE of marina. There have been reports of larger yachts being denied entry to the marina with bad weather approaching. Charted 4 outside hbr in 4·6m on sand/mud needs HM's approval.

marina.name: Marina Porto Atlântico
marina.notes: 200 F&A + 40 €1·64; reception W quay. 2-9·1·5m. Rather oily with limited facilities onsite. HM 0900-1230; 1400-1800. MRCC (21) 4401919; Met 484527 (Porto airport); Police 383649; 951476; Brit Consul 808 203 537; Fire 380018; 391000; HM 953000. Clube de Vela Atlântico 963547. Clube Naval de Leça Bar 951700. Sport Club do Porto 952225.

name: Porto (Oporto)
approachNote: Difficult river ent, prone to swell and fast current; dangerous in strong W'lies (see Passage information for port closure signals). New moles at the ent; see AC 3258 inset. N mole is lit by Fl R 5s 16m 9M (W twr, R stripes); S mole by Fl G 5s 15m 6M. Chan has 2 PHM and 2 SHM bys (lit, numbered 1 to 4). When abeam PHM no 4, head 161°/0·3M to Douro Marina. The bar shifts and charted depths are unreliable, but the N and S bkwtr extensions over recent years have much improved access and shelter. For buoys upriver see Lights, buoys and waypoints.
shelter: Excellent at Douro marina. Also possible at small quay by Cantareira, or at Cais de Estiva between the first and second bridges. A road recce of the Rio Douro is advised.

marina.name: Douro Marina
marina.notes: 240 + 60 €4·30 inc; max LOA <50m. 1M from the ent to the Rio Douro, beware strong current inside marina, particularly on ebb. Friendly staff, English, French & Spanish spoken. Free shuttle bus to large shopping complex; buses to centre of Porto, car/cycle hire available. Many good reports received. Marina position 41°08'·57N 08°39'·02W.

name: Aveiro
approachNote: WPT (off chartlet) Boia Aveiro buoy, 40°37'·86N 08°46'·98W, 056°/1·1M to hbr ent. Caution: tidal streams. From abeam the lt ho the ent to B de San Jacinto is about 9 ca NE. Do not proceed further E. The 060·6° ldg line (marks hard to see) leads between the moles. A military airfield with conspic control twr is NE of the hbr ent. Caution: changes to depths S of main appr reported: an area liable to change exists centred 40°37'·69N 08°46'·00W.
shelter: Good, but the bar (Rptd 10m LWN 2015) becomes impassable in strong onshore winds and the port is often one of the first on this coast to be closed by bad weather or swell, even if there is no wind. Two small marinas usually have space (see Facilities) or at the N end of Baia de San Jacinto in about 7m avoiding the large drying sandbank (buoyed) inside the outer end of the bay's N bkwtr.

marina.name: CVCN YC marina
marina.notes: 3/4 via Mira Canal (2m) and road bridge (14m air draught). Avela YC marina 3/4 422142 www.avela.pt near electrical cable (21m air draught). Port VHF Ch 11, 16 (M-F 0900-1200 and 1400-1700). Brit Consul 808 203 537; HM 393300; www.portodeaveiro.pt; geral@portodeaveiro.pt. English/French-speaking contact is Senhor Cala Barras, Avela YC. A ferry reported to run from near the anchorage in Baia de San Jacinto to Aveiro.

name: Figueira da Foz
approachNote: WPT 40°08'·61N 08°53'·12W (off chartlet) 081°/5ca to hbr ent. The bar in bad weather is the only offshore danger. Cabo Mondego lt ho is 3M NW of hbr ent. Buarcos light is on the beach 1·1M N of hbr ent. Ldg lts lead 081·5° past marina ent. Lts as chartlet and Lights, buoys and waypoints. Extensive beaches N and S of hbr ent and a white suspension bridge (39m cl'nce) 1·5M E of ent are conspic from seaward.
shelter: Excellent in marina on N bank, 0·75M from hbr ent. Bar at ent has charted depth of 4·1m, but this cannot be relied upon. It shifts/shoals constantly and can be highly dangerous in swell, especially Nov-Mar with strong W/NW winds. Conditions on the bar are signalled from mast at Forte de Santa Catarina (N of inner moles) and may be broadcast on VHF Ch 11.

marina.name: Marina Figueira da Foz
marina.notes: 2-8m. 200 inc €2·65, plus possible surcharge for beam, multihulls +50%. Strong ebb tide can affect pontoons nearest marina ent. Reception/fuel berth is dead ahead of ent. Call Capmarfoz. Maritime Police 916 352629; Port VHF Ch 11, 422955.

name: Nazaré
approachNote: WPT 39°35'·69N 09°05'·95W, 106°/1M to hbr ent. Due to an underwater canyon there are depths of 452m and 152m 5M and 7ca offshore respectively. Pontal da Nazaré lt is 1M NNW of hbr ent on a low headland; keep at least 2ca off. Here a conspic wind farm (FR lts & W strobes) is visible in 20-30M in good vis.
shelter: Good with all-weather access. No anchorage in outer harbour.

marina.name: Marina de Nazaré
marina.notes: 968074254 is in the SW corner of the inner hbr, call (VHF) on appr; berth on the hammerheads or outer pontoons, <15m and 3-5m draft. 40+14 €2·45. H24 in NE corner. (80t). Clube Naval de Nazaré 560422. Their pontoons in the NE corner are private but 5 berths are kept for visitors, with more often available if Club members are away (call/email ahead). €2·35 inc; wi-fi. HM Brit Consul 808 203 537; 561116; Port HM 561255; CHF Ch 11 (HO). Town 1½M to the N; Valado dos Frades 7km; Lisboa 125km.

name: Peniche
approachNote: WPT 39°20'·42N 09°22'·53W, 000°/5ca to W mole hd. 3kn speed limit in hbr. The coastal route, a 5M wide chan between Cabo Carvoeiro and Ilha da Berlenga, is often rough. Peniche and the offshore islands are in an 'Area to be avoided', only by ships >30t (for environmental reasons); see Area map. Continued overleaf.
shelter: Good all-weather hbr, but uncomfortable if SW swell works in; also noisy and prone to heavy FV wash. Possible moorings or anchorage in SE part of hbr; holding is good, but ground is foul in places, trip line essential. The anchorage in 4m on N side of peninsula is open to swell, even in S'lies.
